title: Dense Delightful Northern Thai Fish Tagliere
description: A fun and springy Northern Thai-inspired pescatarian-friendly dish made with a flaky tagliere crust and a flavorful fish filling.

ingredients.name: fish fillet (such as cod or tilapia)

instructions: In a small bowl, combine the yeast and warm water. Let it sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, salt, and sugar. Add the vegetable oil and mix until the dough comes together.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch (6 mm).
Place the dough on a 9x13 inch (23x33 cm) baking sheet or tagliere. Let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour until it has doubled in size.
In a separate bowl, mix together the fish fillet, lemongrass, cilantro, fish sauce, lime juice, and garlic.
Place the fish mixture on one half of the dough, leaving a 1/2 inch (1 cm) border around the edges.
Fold the other half of the dough over the fish mixture and press the edges to seal.
Use a knife to cut a few slits in the top of the tagliere to allow steam to escape.
Bake the tagliere in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes until it is golden brown and the fish is cooked through.
Let the tagliere cool for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve warm with your favorite dipping sauce or a side of steamed vegetables.
To store leftovers, let the tagliere cool completely before wrapping it in plastic wrap or aluminum foil and refrigerating it for up to 3 days. or freezing it for up to 2 months.
